_comment: Human-curated country metadata. Quantitative indicators (population, GDP, internet %, etc.) are fetched automatically into countries.json. This file holds things that don't live in APIs: overviews, industries, media outlets, focus areas. Add new countries here to expand coverage; the refresh script will fill in their numbers on the next run.

USA.name: United States of America
USA.overview: The world's largest economy and the primary exporter of global media and digital platforms. Home to the companies that define much of the world's online information environment.

media.top_tv: CBS, NBC, ABC, Fox, CNN, MSNBC
media.top_radio: NPR, iHeartRadio network, SiriusXM
media.top_online_news: New York Times, CNN, Washington Post, Fox News

GBR.name: United Kingdom
GBR.overview: Globally influential media market historically anchored by the BBC. Strong public-service broadcasting tradition sits alongside one of the world's most competitive commercial press markets.

media.top_tv: BBC, ITV, Channel 4, Sky
media.top_radio: BBC Radio (1/2/4), Global Radio (Capital, Heart)
media.top_online_news: BBC News, The Guardian, Mail Online, Sky News

BRA.name: Brazil
BRA.overview: Latin America's largest media market, with television and WhatsApp both playing outsized roles in news distribution. Portuguese-language cohesion makes it one of the most unified large media markets in the world.

media.top_tv: Globo, SBT, Record, Band
media.top_radio: Jovem Pan, Bandeirantes, CBN
media.top_online_news: G1 (Globo), UOL, Folha de Sao Paulo, Estadao

NGA.name: Nigeria
NGA.overview: Africa's most populous country and the continent's largest media and entertainment market. Home to Nollywood, Afrobeats, and some of Africa's most influential digital journalism platforms.

media.top_tv: NTA, Channels TV, Arise News, TVC
media.top_radio: Wazobia FM, Cool FM, BBC Hausa
media.top_online_news: Premium Times, Sahara Reporters, Punch, Vanguard

KEN.name: Kenya
KEN.overview: East Africa's digital hub and a pioneer of mobile money (M-Pesa). A vibrant independent press coexists with government pressure that has grown in recent years.

media.top_tv: Citizen TV, KTN, NTV, K24
media.top_radio: Radio Citizen, Radio Maisha, Classic 105
media.top_online_news: Nation.Africa, Standard Media, Tuko, The Star

IND.name: India
IND.overview: The world's most populous country and its fastest-growing major media market. Extraordinary linguistic diversity drives a multi-language news ecosystem unmatched anywhere else.

media.top_tv: Doordarshan, Star India, Zee, Sun TV, Republic, NDTV
media.top_radio: All India Radio, Radio Mirchi, Red FM
media.top_online_news: Times of India, Hindustan Times, The Hindu, NDTV.com

CHN.name: China
CHN.overview: The world's second-largest economy and a uniquely self-contained digital ecosystem. Major global platforms (Google, Facebook, Twitter) are inaccessible; domestic super-apps dominate every aspect of media consumption.

media.top_tv: CCTV, provincial satellite channels
media.top_radio: China National Radio (CNR)
media.top_online_news: People's Daily, Xinhua, Sina, Toutiao

IDN.name: Indonesia
IDN.capital: Jakarta (Nusantara being developed as successor capital)
IDN.overview: The world's fourth-most-populous country, spread across 17,000+ islands. One of the world's heaviest social-media-using populations, with TikTok and Instagram central to daily media diets.

media.top_tv: SCTV, Indosiar, RCTI, TransTV, MetroTV
media.top_radio: RRI, Prambors, Elshinta
media.top_online_news: Detik.com, Kompas.com, Tribunnews, CNN Indonesia

DEU.name: Germany
DEU.overview: Europe's largest economy, with one of the world's strongest public-service broadcasters (ARD and ZDF) funded by a household media levy. High trust in traditional news relative to most peer democracies.

media.top_tv: ARD, ZDF, RTL, ProSieben, Sat.1
media.top_radio: Deutschlandfunk, regional ARD stations, Antenne Bayern
media.top_online_news: Bild, Der Spiegel, Sueddeutsche Zeitung, Tagesschau

MEX.name: Mexico
MEX.overview: Latin America's second-largest economy, with a media market historically dominated by a small number of large broadcasters. One of the most dangerous countries in the world to practice journalism.

media.top_tv: Televisa, TV Azteca, Imagen TV
media.top_radio: Grupo Formula, Radio Centro, MVS Radio
media.top_online_news: El Universal, Milenio, Reforma, Animal Politico

PAK.name: Pakistan
PAK.overview: South Asia's second-most-populous country and one of the world's largest Urdu-language media markets. Television remains dominant for news; private cable news channels have proliferated since the 2000s liberalisation.

media.top_tv: Geo News, ARY News, Dunya News, PTV, Samaa TV
media.top_radio: Radio Pakistan (PBC), FM100, FM101, Mast FM
media.top_online_news: Dawn.com, Geo.tv, The News International, Express Tribune

BGD.name: Bangladesh
BGD.overview: South Asia's most densely populated country and one of its fastest-growing mobile internet markets. Bangla-language news dominates domestic consumption, and Facebook functions as the de facto public square.

media.top_tv: BTV (state), Channel i, ATN Bangla, Somoy TV, Jamuna TV
media.top_radio: Bangladesh Betar (state), Radio Foorti, ABC Radio
media.top_online_news: Prothom Alo, The Daily Star, bdnews24, Kaler Kantho

RUS.name: Russia
RUS.overview: A geographically vast media market where state-aligned television still drives national news reach. Western social platforms have been largely blocked since 2022; VKontakte and Telegram now anchor online conversation.

media.top_tv: Channel One (Pervyi), Rossiya-1, NTV, REN TV
media.top_radio: Radio Rossii, Vesti FM, Mayak, Business FM
media.top_online_news: RIA Novosti, TASS, Lenta.ru, RBC, Meduza (in exile)

ETH.name: Ethiopia
ETH.overview: Africa's second-most-populous country and host of the African Union. A state-dominated broadcasting landscape coexists with rapidly growing diaspora-run digital media, especially during periods of domestic conflict.

media.top_tv: EBC (state), Fana, Walta, Kana TV
media.top_radio: EBC Radio, Sheger FM, Fana FM
media.top_online_news: Addis Standard, Ethiopian News Agency, Borkena, Capital Ethiopia

JPN.name: Japan
JPN.overview: One of the world's most print-intensive media markets — the Yomiuri Shimbun remains the largest-circulation paid daily on earth. Television and newspapers retain high trust, while X (Twitter) dominates real-time news discussion.

media.top_tv: NHK (public), Nippon TV, TV Asahi, TBS, Fuji TV, TV Tokyo
media.top_radio: NHK Radio, J-WAVE, TOKYO FM, Nippon Cultural Broadcasting
media.top_online_news: Yomiuri, Asahi, Nikkei, Mainichi, NHK News Web

PHL.name: Philippines
PHL.overview: Routinely ranked among the world's heaviest social-media-using populations. Facebook functions as the primary news source for most Filipinos, making the platform central to every election cycle.

media.top_tv: GMA, ABS-CBN (restricted), TV5, PTV (state), CNN Philippines
media.top_radio: DZBB, DZMM, DZRH
media.top_online_news: Rappler, Inquirer.net, GMA News Online, ABS-CBN News

EGY.name: Egypt
EGY.overview: The Arab world's largest media producer and home of Al-Ahram, the region's oldest major daily. State-aligned satellite broadcasting dominates, while independent journalism faces significant constraints.
languages: Arabic (the Egyptian dialect carries broad regional influence)

media.top_tv: ERTU (state), ON TV, DMC, Al Hayat, CBC
media.top_radio: ERTU Radio, Nogoum FM, Nile FM
media.top_online_news: Al-Ahram, Youm7, Al-Masry Al-Youm, Mada Masr (blocked domestically)

COD.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
COD.overview: Francophone Africa's most populous country. Radio remains the most widely-reaching medium given vast geography and uneven electricity access, while urban youth drive rapidly-growing mobile internet adoption.

media.top_tv: RTNC (state), Digital Congo, Molière TV, B-One
media.top_radio: Radio Okapi (UN-backed), RTNC Radio, Top Congo FM
media.top_online_news: Actualite.cd, Radio Okapi, 7sur7.cd, Politico.cd

VNM.name: Vietnam
VNM.overview: A tightly regulated media environment under a single-party state, paired with one of Asia's most digitally active populations. Zalo, a domestic messaging app, competes with global platforms for daily user time.

media.top_tv: VTV (state), HTV, VTC
media.top_radio: VOV (Voice of Vietnam)
media.top_online_news: VnExpress, Tuoi Tre, Thanh Nien, Vietnamnet

IRN.name: Iran
IRN.overview: A state-controlled broadcast landscape running parallel to a sprawling shadow ecosystem of VPN-accessed platforms. Satellite channels from diaspora outlets reach broad audiences despite legal restrictions.

media.top_tv: IRIB (state), Manoto (London-based), Iran International (London-based)
media.top_radio: IRIB Radio, Radio Farda (VOA, blocked domestically)
media.top_online_news: Tasnim, Fars, ISNA, IRNA, BBC Persian (blocked)

TUR.name: Türkiye
TUR.overview: Straddling Europe and Asia, with a media landscape reshaped by heavy ownership consolidation under government-aligned conglomerates. YouTube and X remain critical channels for opposition voices.

media.top_tv: TRT (state), ATV, Show TV, Kanal D, Fox Türkiye, NTV, Halk TV
media.top_radio: TRT FM, Radyo D, Kral FM
media.top_online_news: Hürriyet, Sabah, Sözcü, T24, BBC Türkçe

THA.name: Thailand
THA.overview: A mobile-first media market where LINE, Facebook, and TikTok dominate daily news consumption. Royal defamation (lèse-majesté) laws significantly shape what can be reported and debated.

media.top_tv: Thai PBS, Channel 3, Channel 7, Thai Rath TV, Workpoint
media.top_radio: MCOT Radio, Thai PBS Radio
media.top_online_news: Thairath, Matichon, Bangkok Post, Khaosod

FRA.name: France
FRA.overview: A UN Security Council permanent member and one of Europe's largest media markets. Public-service broadcasting is influential, and state subsidies for pluralistic print journalism are among the world's highest.

media.top_tv: France 2, TF1, M6, BFM TV, CNews, France 3
media.top_radio: France Inter, RTL, Europe 1, France Info
media.top_online_news: Le Monde, Le Figaro, Libération, Mediapart

ZAF.name: South Africa
ZAF.overview: The continent's most developed media infrastructure and an English-language publishing hub for Africa. Constitutional press-freedom protections are strong; economic pressures on newsrooms are acute.

media.top_tv: SABC (public), eTV, MultiChoice (DStv), Newzroom Afrika
media.top_radio: SAfm, Ukhozi FM, Metro FM, 702, Radio 2000
media.top_online_news: News24, Daily Maverick, TimesLIVE, Mail & Guardian, IOL

ITA.name: Italy
ITA.overview: A television-centric media market where the legacy of the Berlusconi-era Mediaset empire still shapes prime-time news. Regional newspapers remain an important news source for many Italians.

media.top_tv: RAI (public), Mediaset (Canale 5, Italia 1, Rete 4), La7, Sky TG24
media.top_radio: Rai Radio, RTL 102.5, Radio Deejay, Radio 24
media.top_online_news: Corriere della Sera, La Repubblica, La Stampa, Il Sole 24 Ore, ANSA

COL.name: Colombia
COL.overview: One of Latin America's most dynamic media markets, with strong investigative journalism traditions. Reporters operating in conflict-affected rural regions face continuing safety risks.

media.top_tv: Caracol TV, RCN, Canal 1, Señal Colombia
media.top_radio: Caracol Radio, RCN Radio, W Radio, Blu Radio
media.top_online_news: El Tiempo, El Espectador, Semana, La Silla Vacía

ARG.name: Argentina
ARG.overview: Latin America's third-largest economy and historically one of its most newspaper-rich democracies. A deeply polarised political press is balanced by very high social-media engagement.

media.top_tv: Telefe, El Trece, América TV, TV Pública, Canal 9
media.top_radio: Radio Nacional, Radio Mitre, La 100, Radio 10
media.top_online_news: Clarín, La Nación, Infobae, Página 12

CAN.name: Canada
CAN.overview: A bilingual media landscape with CBC/Radio-Canada as a central public-service institution. Proximity to the US drives high consumption of American content alongside domestic journalism in both English and French.

media.top_tv: CBC / Radio-Canada, CTV, Global, TVA, Citytv
media.top_radio: CBC Radio One, ICI Première (Radio-Canada), Chorus stations
media.top_online_news: CBC News, Globe and Mail, Toronto Star, National Post, La Presse, Le Devoir

SAU.name: Saudi Arabia
SAU.overview: The Arab world's largest economy and a major funder of pan-Arab media via MBC, Al Arabiya, and related groups. State-aligned broadcasters set much of the region's daily news agenda.

media.top_tv: Saudi TV (state), MBC 1, Al Arabiya, Rotana
media.top_radio: Saudi Radio, MBC FM
media.top_online_news: Al Arabiya, Asharq Al-Awsat, Arab News (English), Al Riyadh

AUS.name: Australia
AUS.overview: A concentrated media market long dominated by a handful of legacy conglomerates (News Corp, Nine, Seven). The 2021 News Media Bargaining Code shaped global debates over Big Tech and journalism funding.

media.top_tv: ABC (public), Seven, Nine, Ten, SBS
media.top_radio: ABC Radio, ARN network, SCA network, Triple J
media.top_online_news: ABC News, news.com.au, Sydney Morning Herald, The Guardian Australia, The Australian
